name: Ascent to Campo López from Lorca — return via the Sierra de Almenara
name_original: Ascensión a Campo López desde Lorca — vuelta por la Sierra de Almenara
region_intro: Road cycling around Lorca, a historic city in the Region of Murcia, offers diverse landscapes and varied terrain. The area features rolling hills, mountain passes, and significant natural parks like Sierra Espuña, providing a range of road cycling routes. Cyclists can find everything from gently rolling terrain to demanding ascents, often with vistas of mountains, valleys, and forests. The region also includes coastal areas and is nestled in a valley surrounded by mountains. Best road cycling routes around Lorca The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de la Torrecilla y Puerto Lumbreras — circular from Lorca, a 40.0 miles (64.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the mountain ranges surrounding Lorca. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ascent to Campo López from Lorca — return via the Sierra de Almenara, a moderate 36.1 miles (58.0 km) path. This route features an ascent to a viewpoint and a return through the Sierra de Almenara. Local road cyclists also love the La Fuentsanta and La Paca via the Puentes Reservoir — circular from Lorca, a 47.5 miles (76.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the Puentes Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Road cycling around Lorca is defined by rolling hills, mountain passes, and coastal areas. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Lorca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 2500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Lorca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Lorca?
A: Lorca and its surroundings offer a wide variety of road cycling routes. There are over 130 routes available, catering to different skill levels. You can find approximately 30 easy routes, 60 moderate routes, and 40 difficult routes to explore.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Lorca?
A: Lorca benefits from a mild, dry climate, making it suitable for road cycling throughout much of the year. The winter months are particularly appealing when other regions might be less accessible. April and October are also popular months for riding, offering pleasant conditions for exploring the diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Lorca?
A: The region around Lorca offers diverse terrain, from gently rolling hills to demanding ascents and steep mountain passes. You'll find routes through the lush Sierra Espuña Natural Park, along coastal areas, and nestled in valleys surrounded by mountains. This variety provides options for all types of road cyclists.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced road cyclists will find plenty of challenging routes around Lorca, particularly within the Sierra Espuña Natural Park. This area features several long and enjoyable climbs, such as the notable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2953991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Bermejo&lt;/a&gt;, which offers significant elevation gain and a high-mountain feel. Approximately 40 difficult routes are available for those seeking a demanding ride.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Lorca?
A: Road cycling routes around Lorca often pass through stunning natural features and historical landmarks. You can explore the lush Sierra Espuña Natural Park, with its varied ecosystems and breathtaking vistas. Some routes also lead to coastal areas like the Parque Regional de Cabo Cope - Puntas de Calnegre. Historic sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4085357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cocedores Beach&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/998553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of Wolves&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Lorca?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Lorca are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009705187/sierra-de-la-torrecilla-y-puerto-lumbreras-circular-from-lorca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Torrecilla y Puerto Lumbreras — circular from Lorca&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route exploring the mountain ranges. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010134954/la-fuentsanta-and-la-paca-via-the-puentes-reservoir-circular-from-lorca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fuentsanta and La Paca via the Puentes Reservoir — circular from Lorca&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through varied terrain.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through charming villages or offer cafe stops?
A: Absolutely. Many road cycling routes in Lorca pass through small, picturesque villages such as El Berro, Gebas, and Casas Nuevas. These villages offer opportunities to stop, experience local gastronomy, and find cafes for a refreshing break. The 'Espuroad' initiative within Sierra Espuña also connects unique places within the park, often suitable for such stops.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Lorca?
A: The road cycling experience in Lorca is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from rolling hills and mountain passes to coastal areas, and the peaceful, relatively car-free roads, especially in Sierra Espuña. The variety of routes catering to all ability levels is also a significant draw.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for families?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, some options are suitable for families, particularly those looking for less demanding rides. Within Sierra Espuña, the 'Espuroad' initiative offers several road routes, typically around 80 kilometers, that border the mountains and connect unique places within the park, some of which are suitable for families. It's best to check the difficulty grade and elevation profile for specific routes.

Q: Are there any routes that offer coastal views?
A: Yes, Lorca's proximity to the coast means you can find road cycling routes that offer stunning coastal views. The region includes the Parque Regional de Cabo Cope - Puntas de Calnegre, which features numerous beaches and coves. While some areas are less accessible, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009097132/ascent-to-campo-lopez-from-lorca-return-via-the-sierra-de-almenara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Campo López from Lorca — return via the Sierra de Almenara&lt;/a&gt; can provide glimpses of the coastal landscape as you return through the Sierra de Almenara.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling trails in Lorca?
A: Lorca, being a city, offers various parking options. For routes starting directly from Lorca, you can typically find public parking within the city. For routes venturing into natural parks like Sierra Espuña, designated parking areas are usually available at key access points or visitor centers. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions for recommended starting points and parking information.
tips: From Morata to Campo López there is a beautiful road with little traffic, curves and uphill. Shortly before reaching Campo López is this stop from which you can see the Guadalentín Valley and Sierra Espuña in the background.
OK not quite a forest, but plenty of trees, which is rare for these parts.  Good climb starting from Águilas.  Worth it for the views and the rarity of tree cover. Nice quiet roads too.
Beautiful views during this section of the road in the Region of Murcia located in the Sierra de Almenara. A paradise for road bike lovers among cultivated fields.
